mnemonics: SPACE: Strategies, Payoffs, Algorithms, Computation, Equilibrium. This spells SPACE, reminding you of the continuous 'space' of strategies in Algorithmic Game Theory.
keyPoints: The focus is on computing equilibria (like Nash) in games with continuous strategy sets (e.g., any real number between 0 and 1).
Algorithms often involve solving systems of equations or inequalities derived from players' best-response conditions.
Computational complexity is a major concern; it's important to know which problems are tractable (P) and which are intractable (NP-hard).
commonMistakes: Confusing discrete game analysis (with a finite set of moves) with continuous game analysis (which uses calculus and optimization).
Forgetting to check the second-order conditions (e.g., concavity) when using first-order conditions to find a best response or equilibrium.
